fields.description:
# RC2b: snapshot-horizon-safe replay (no silent sub-horizon drop)

## Why
When two clones write at once, one can compact and delete the events it folded before seeing the other's
event. After merge, that event sorts BEFORE the snapshot and replay's positional skip drops it regardless of
`source_event_uuids` -> silent data loss. Live store holds ~11 such orphans.

## Decision (ACCEPTED): Option 1 (rebuild-on-stray) + Option 3 (conservative horizon)
Option 2 (per-field LWW-by-timestamp) deferred as a possible follow-up (biggest blast radius).

## Concrete rules (resolving the previously-deferred design)
- SETTLED (Option 3): compaction folds an event only if `now_hlc - event_hlc >= COMPACTION_HORIZON`, a NEW
  configurable constant (default 1800s = 30 min, documented as ">= 2x worst-case clone sync latency") AND the
  clone completed >=1 successful `git fetch origin/tickets` this pass. Younger "hot-edge" events stay live
  `.json` for the next cycle. This gates which events `compact.py` folds.
- REBUILD (Option 1): fsck detects a merged-in pre-snapshot orphan (KNOWN-type `.json` sorting before the
  SNAPSHOT, uuid absent from `source_event_uuids`) and rebuilds that ticket's snapshot from the full ordered
  log INCLUDING `.retired` (a `reduce_ticket(..., include_retired=True)` path; reduce_ticket today EXCLUDES
  `.retired`). Rebuild writes `<snap>.tmp` + atomic rename; old snapshot -> `.bak`, deleted only after a clean
  replay round-trip. Idempotent + resumable (re-run on a correct snapshot is a no-op). Log (WARN) each detected
  orphan + each rebuild; expose a rebuild counter. Concurrency: rebuild only when quiesced OR re-check the
  event log after rebuild and redo if a new event landed.

## Dependencies (explicit — not phantom)
- A1 = tricolour-head-ratfish delivers the `.retired` rename + `RETIRED_SUFFIX`/`is_active_event` (depends_on, wired).
- A3 = entitled-unsweet-nutria REUSES the rebuild path authored here.

## Scope
`reducer/_processors.py` (process_snapshot + positional skip ~648-702), `reducer/_sort.py`, `reducer/_api.py`
(include-retired rebuild), `_commands/compact.py` (conservative-horizon fold guard). NOT the reconciler code.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `COMPACTION_HORIZON` constant defined + documented (default 1800s, configurable); ADR records the rule.
- [ ] Unit test: an event younger than the horizon is NOT folded (absent from `source_event_uuids`, left live).
- [ ] Rebuild-on-stray path (atomic tmp+rename, `.bak`, idempotent, logged) rebuilds from the full ordered log incl. `.retired`.
- [ ] A merged-in pre-horizon orphan (uuid not in `source_event_uuids`) is no longer silently dropped.
- [ ] Two-clone sub-horizon-append data-loss regression test: RED before, GREEN after.
- [ ] Determinism proof: replay twice on the same file set (incl. shuffled directory-listing order) -> byte-identical compiled state.
- [ ] Decision + design recorded in an ADR; `.retired` retention/GC follow-up acknowledged (shared with A1).
